The slide suggests using a top-down (goal-directed) parser as a possible solution to rule out bad sentences. This approach involves starting to build a constituent based on the needs of the left context rather than the input, preventing the construction of constituents that cannot be used, such as building "spoon" as a verb when there's no context to use a verb there.
